data.description:
## Story D: `reinstall-hooks.sh` installs on any supported stack (not just Python)

### Description
**What**: Remove the hard-fail-on-missing-pyproject.toml check in `reinstall-hooks.sh` and replace it with a stack-aware precondition that accepts `pyproject.toml` OR `Gemfile` OR `package.json`. Missing all three produces an actionable error, not a silent skip.
**Why**: Today Ruby and Node projects cannot install DSO hooks because `reinstall-hooks.sh` refuses to proceed without a `pyproject.toml` — an invariant that never made sense for non-Python stacks.
**Scope**:
- IN: precondition check replaced, `[DSO ERROR]` with actionable message when all three signal files are missing, existing Python project behavior unchanged
- OUT: per-stack hook variants, hook-behavior differences across stacks (hooks themselves are stack-agnostic once installed)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, running `reinstall-hooks.sh` in a Ruby-only project (Gemfile present, no pyproject.toml) completes successfully and installs hooks
  ← Satisfies: "reinstall-hooks.sh removes the hard-fail on missing pyproject.toml; installs hooks on any supported stack"
- When this story is complete, running `reinstall-hooks.sh` in a project with none of {pyproject.toml, Gemfile, package.json} exits non-zero with a message listing the accepted signal files
  ← Satisfies: epic Scenario Analysis "reinstall-hooks.sh removal of pyproject.toml check inadvertently breaks a Python-project invariant"
- When this story is complete, running `reinstall-hooks.sh` in an existing Python-only project produces identical behavior to pre-change
  ← Satisfies: regression-free change

### Considerations
- [Maintainability] Precondition logic shared with detect-stack.sh; consider extracting a helper so the list of accepted signal files is defined once
- [Testing] Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a Ruby or Node developer, I can install DSO hooks in my project without a pyproject.toml
